notes: [audio] assault.objective.decrease_and_score: QC MUTATOR_HOOKFUNCTION(as, PlayHitsound) returns true for a func_assault_destructible victim (forces the standard hitsound when shelling a wall); the port doesn't special-case it. Parity-neutral but not explicitly modeled (now tracked as new_unmapped assault.notify.play_hitsound).

[logic] bot-ai.population.namemodel: skin/shirt/pants columns and the 12 per-bot skill-modifier columns of bots.txt are still not parsed (name+model+forced-team only); those drive the skill-modifier and player-color gaps elsewhere.

[values] bot-ai.think.throttle: Simulated bot ping/aim-latency (CS(this).ping, bot_aimlatency) is not modeled; bots have zero network latency, so their lead/reaction is slightly sharper than stock at low skill.
[values] bot-ai.aim.fire: Long-range fire-hesitation chance still omits the bot_aggresskill modifier (port uses skill only), so per-bot aggression variance is lost. (UNCHANGED.)

[logic/values] bot-ai.aim.lead: Ballistic aim is a closed-form gravity-drop approximation, not QC's 10-trace tracetoss search; lobbed-weapon aim (mortar/nade) is less accurate, especially with obstacles in the arc, and never traces the world to confirm the toss lands. (UNCHANGED.) ; Shot speed is read from g_balance_<wep>_primary_speed/gravity for the single ChosenWeapon, not per-weaponentity with W_WeaponSpeedFactor applied; weapons without those cvars are treated as hitscan (no lead). (UNCHANGED.)

[logic] bot-ai.target.chooseenemy: No second see-through-transparent (DPCONTENTS_OPAQUE) scan pass and no misc_breakablemodel secondary targeting; bots won't shoot breakables or acquire targets through transparent surfaces. (UNCHANGED — Wave-2 did not add the second-scan loop.)

[logic/values] bot-ai.weapon.choose: Weapon picking is a hitscan-at-range / splash-up-close bucket choosing the highest-impulse owned weapon, not the QC ordered far/mid/close priority lists. (UNCHANGED.) ; No weapon-combo behaviour (bot_ai_weapon_combo): bots don't hold a weapon for a follow-up combo shot. (UNCHANGED.) ; No bot_rangepreference shift, so per-bot 'sniper vs spammer' weapon-range personalities are absent. (UNCHANGED.)

[logic/liveness] bot-ai.weapon.wr_aim: Only the primary attack is ever driven (ButtonAttack2 is hardcoded false at Emit, BotBrain.cs:374): the per-weapon wr_aim — SECONDARY fire, charge-up/release, projectile detonation and weapon combos — is never invoked, so bots never use alt-fire or detonate/combo. (UNCHANGED by Waves 1-2.) ; Because wr_aim is bypassed, weapon combos and the lastfiredweapon bookkeeping never happen; bots fire only the primary. (UNCHANGED.)

[logic/values] bot-ai.roles.goalrating: Goal rating still uses straight-line inverse distance (f*rangebias/(rangebias+vlen(dist))) instead of the QC waypoint-cost (Dijkstra) field; bots can prefer a goal near in a straight line but far by path. (UNCHANGED — GoalRater.Rate at BotRoles.cs:58 is still vlen.) ; rangebias is still hardcoded 2000 for every rate call (items/enemies/roam) instead of the per-rating-call bias QC uses. (UNCHANGED.) ; No separate _movingtarget (5.5s) strategy interval for goals that can move. (UNCHANGED.) ; enemy

[logic/values] bot-ai.move.steer: The brain's danger-check JumpStepHeight (BotNavigation const = 48) is still a hardcoded constant, and is even inconsistent with Base's jumpstepheightvec (stepheight + jumpheight_vec*0.85 ≈ 70 @ stock) and with the live Steer reach (StepHeightLive + JumpHeightApex, missing the ×0.85). Non-default physics adjusts the Steer obstacle-jump but not this const. ; QC's full movetogoal handling of teleport/ladder/box-volume waypoints, water swim-up, and the per-goal stop/turn logic is partially collapsed

[logic] bot-ai.move.danger: Danger evade is still brake-only: the QC lateral evadedanger vector (steer along the safe edge, distance scaled by skill) is collapsed into a reverse-velocity brake, so bots back straight off a ledge instead of sidestepping along it. (UNCHANGED.) ; Per-waypoint danger-cost ROUTING is inert: Wave-2 added Waypoint.Danger + made A* add nb.Danger, but no botframe_updatedangerousobjects producer ever SETS Danger (it is always 0), so routing still does not avoid chronically dangerous waypoints — the c

[logic/values/timing] bot-ai.combat.dodge: Combat movement is invented (periodic strafe-direction flip + health-advantage close/flee bias), not QC havocbot_dodge which sidesteps specific incoming projectiles by their flight path. (UNCHANGED.) ; The SUPERBOT random-direction combat jitter (havocbot_movetogoal:1280-1307) is not reproduced; only the generic strafe stands in. (UNCHANGED.)

[logic/values/timing] bot-ai.special.jetpack_rocketjump: Jetpack navigation (take off / fly to point / brake-land) is missing: bots can't cross jetpack-only gaps even with bot_ai_navigation_jetpack on. (UNCHANGED.) ; Skill>6 trigger_hurt escape (jetpack up, or Devastator rocketjump if HP allows) is missing: bots die in hurt volumes they could have escaped. (UNCHANGED.)

[logic/values/timing] bot-ai.move.keyboard: Keyboard-movement emulation missing: port bots emit a fully analog wish-move at all skills, so they strafe/turn more precisely than stock bots below skill 10. (UNCHANGED.)

[logic/values/timing] bot-ai.scripting.commands: Bot scripting entirely missing: the bot_cmd command queue does nothing, so map/console bot scripts, cutscene-driven bots, and g_waypointeditor bot driving are inert. (UNCHANGED.)

[logic/values/timing/liveness] bot-waypoints.graph.autolink: No PVS cull: Base culls candidate links by checkpvs before tracewalking; the port does distance + tracewalk only (slower, may keep links Base culls). ; No crouch<->normal short-link special case (Base caps those at 100qu, not 1050qu). ; Box / JUMP / SUPPORT directional link forbidding not replicated: Base forbids specific outgoing/incoming directions for boxes, JUMP and SUPPORT (waypoints.qc:1212-1237); the port skips teleport/jump/ladder/custom/support sources wholesale instead.

[logic] bot-waypoints.file.load_hardwired: Hardwired links are marked with the CustomJp flag as a stand-in (no dedicated wphwXX mirror / hardwired concept), so links cannot be identified for save/round-trip; the special-link '*'-prefix is loaded the same as a normal link rather than getting Base's special-link source-type filter.
[logic/values/timing] bot-waypoints.file.save: Port is a read-only consumer: no .waypoints/.cache/.hardwired file writers, so authored graphs cannot be saved or round-tripped.

[logic/values/timing/liveness] bot-waypoints.auto.spawnforitem: Only runs in the no-.waypoints-file auto-generate path; on maps that ship a .waypoints file the port does NOT inject runtime item/teleporter waypoints (Base always does waypoint_spawnforitem at item spawn, snapping to the item). ; bot_waypoints_for_items (= g_waypoints_for_items) gate not honored; floor-snapping uses a fixed +24 z offset rather than Base's waypoint_fixorigin tracedown.

[logic/values/liveness] bot-waypoints.auto.spawnforteleporter: No warpzone-angle math (Base makevectors(warpzone_angles) src/dest projection) and no oblique-warpzone JUMP flag; jumppad push-time cost is approximated (Base uses trigger_push_get_push_time / the auto-link cost), the port uses the plain travel cost. ; Generated-graph path only; shipped maps use authored teleport waypoints from the file.

[logic] bot-waypoints.path.findnearest: No jumppad-fallback (Base returns a reachable jumppad's nearest waypoint when no plain waypoint is reachable); no walkfromwp (walk-TO vs walk-FROM) direction distinction in the reachability tracewalk.

[logic] bot-waypoints.path.routetogoal: Teleport-goal forcing (push wp00 destination, set goalentity), dynamic/moving-target shortcut (bot_ai_strategyinterval_movingtarget 5.5) and the item incoming-link walkability cache are simplified/absent; the port pushes the A* node chain + goal directly.

[logic] bot-waypoints.steer.movetogoal: Obstacle avoidance is a single forward step/jump-height tracebox probe rather than Base's steerlib trident/funnel sweep (see bot-waypoints.steer.steerlib); lateral evade is folded into a straight -velocity brake (BotBrain do_break), a documented simplification of Base's AI_STATUS_DANGER_AHEAD sidestep.

[logic] bot-waypoints.steer.bunnyhop: Base forbids bunnyhop when evadedanger || do_break is set this frame (havocbot.qc:1315); the port computes Bunnyhop inside Steer (BotNavigation.cs:318) before the BotBrain danger brake runs, so a braking bot can still have WantJump set from bunnyhop. The Steer-internal ledge brake (BotNavigation.cs:289-301) is separate from the BotBrain do_break/evadedanger gate and is not consulted by Bunnyhop.

[logic/values] bot-waypoints.steer.steerlib: No steerlib library: flocking/swarm/traceavoid/beamsteer primitives absent (group flocking, the trident/funnel traceavoid obstacle steering, and beamsteer). Bots do not flock and use the simpler single-probe avoidance in Steer + the BotDanger fall probe.

[logic/values/timing/presentation] bot-waypoints.editor: No in-game waypoint editor: cannot place/remove/hardwire/mirror waypoints (g_waypointeditor*); no editor visualization (waypoint model, link lightning beams, colormod by flag). ; No runtime auto-waypointing toward players (botframe_autowaypoints) or useless-waypoint pruning (botframe_deleteuselesswaypoints): the loaded graph is static.

[audio] ctf.capture_shield: No physical ctf_captureshield push entity (no shield model, no force push, no shielded centerprints) — the status is computed and gates pickups + reaches the HUD, but the visible/pushing shield is absent.
[audio] fx-sounds.emit.play2_family: play2 (per-client 2D send) and play2team (per-team 2D send) still have no port equivalent.

[logic] mapobject-func.door.rotating: DOOR_ROTATING_BIDIR / BIDIR_IN_DOWN trigger-side reverse-open logic (door_use's trigger.trigger_reverse path: set lip=666 + pos2=-pos2, and the BIDIR_IN_DOWN re-open guard) is STILL NOT ported; DoorUse still takes (self, actor) with no trigger arg (Doors.cs:383), so lip is never set to 666. Bidirectional rotating doors always open the default direction. Explicitly documented out-of-scope at Doors.cs:15. ; shares the (now-LIVE) reset + CSQC gaps of mapobject-func.door.slide; inherits its NOSPLASH

[presentation] mapobject-func.button: the pressed-state alternate texture (Frame=1) is set on the entity but whether the brush model renders the alt frame in-engine is unverified (presentation). ; CORRECTED 2026-06-25: button_reset round-restart re-arm is LIVE — ButtonReset is assigned at Buttons.cs:42 and dispatched by GameWorld.ResetMapObjects (GameWorld.cs:3887). The prior gap note claiming it was 'STILL dead' / the comment is wrong was stale; the Buttons.cs:15 comment is correct. (See mapobject-func.reset.)

[presentation] mapobject-func.plat: plat_reset initial placement is live + faithful (targeted->raised, untargeted->bottom); the round-restart .reset is ALSO live now — PlatReset is assigned at Platforms.cs:82 and dispatched by GameWorld.ResetMapObjects. (CORRECTED 2026-06-25; the prior 'STILL NOT wired' note was stale — see mapobject-func.reset.) ; plat_outside_touch (the CSQC-side outside trigger that sends a top plat down when a player leaves) is not ported — it is gated to CSQC in Base; minor. ; no CSQC ENT_CLIENT_PLAT draw/net

[presentation/audio] mapobject-func.train: no func_train reset (QC explicitly has `// TODO make a reset function for this one` — so this is faithful to Base's own omission, unlike door/plat/button). ; train moving-sound stop (stopsoundto MSG_BROADCAST on arrival) is not done; the moving noise plays but is not explicitly stopped at each dwell (cosmetic). Port uses SoundChannel.Voice vs QC ATTEN_IDLE (cosmetic). ; no CSQC ENT_CLIENT_TRAIN draw/networking.
[audio] mapobject-func.bobbing: the spawn-time soundto(MSG_INIT) looping ambient is not resent per-player; played through the facade where present.
[audio] mapobject-func.pendulum: ambient .noise not resent per-player (same reduction as bobbing).
[audio] mapobject-func.fourier: looping ambient .noise resent per-player (soundto MSG_INIT) reduced to a single facade Play.
[audio] mapobject-func.vectormamamam: looping ambient .noise per-player resend reduced to facade Play in setactive.

[presentation] mapobject-misc.keys.item_key: item_key pickup centerprint ('You've picked up the X key!') is silently dropped: MapMover.Centerprint(toucher, this.Message) IS called (MapObjectsRegistry.cs:509) but MapObjectsCommon.CenterprintHandler (declared MapObjectsCommon.cs:816) is never ASSIGNED anywhere in game/ or src/ (grep 'CenterprintHandler =' = 0 hits), so Centerprint no-ops on the live path. ; item_keys_names[lowestbit] save (for keylist display strings) and the Q3COMPAT_COMMON QL +8z origin nudge are omitted (no Q3-compat glob

[logic/values/timing/presentation] mapobject-misc.bgmscript.adsr: No bgmscript ADSR envelope: a func_clientwall / func_pointparticles carrying a .bgmscript key does not animate its alpha / origin / angles / emission to the map's .bgs music script — the prop is static instead of pulsing to the soundtrack. doBGMScript / BGMScript_InitEntity / the .bgs loader are absent.

[logic/values/presentation] mapobject-misc.models.clientwall_fade: func_clientwall distance-fade is not rendered: fade_start/fade_end/alpha_max/alpha_min/fade_vertical_offset are parsed onto the Entity (MapObjectFieldsExtra.cs) but NO renderer applies Ent_Wall_PreDraw's distance-fade alpha formula or its PVS alpha-cull — a clientwall meant to fade out with distance stays fully opaque. (The ClientWorld.cs:1234 ItemDistanceAlpha fade is the unrelated item-despawn fade; FadeEndDist is otherwise only read by WeatherSystem for rain/snow draw distance.) ; The antiwal

[audio] mapobject-movers-platforms.plat.q3compat_defaults: CPMA sound_start/sound_end + the pt1_strt/pt1_end default-file probe are not ported (require GetField_fullspawndata / FindFileInMapPack, unavailable headless). The medplat default still applies, so a Q3 plat is audible and correct — only the CPMA per-mapper sound OVERRIDE keys are skipped. Sole residual gap on this row; audio marked partial for it.

[liveness] mapobject-movers-platforms.plat.outside_target_use: PlatOutsideTouch is implemented and faithful but DEAD — and that is itself faithful to Base: Base platforms.qc only DEFINES plat_outside_touch (line 111) and never installs/spawns an outside trigger anywhere, so stock func_plat never calls it. The port matches (no outside trigger generated). Reachable only via custom-mapper wiring neither Base nor the port produces.

[logic] mapobject-target.items.use: The spawnfunc's normalized re-serialization of item tokens into a max/min/minus-prefixed give string (driven by spawnflags 0/1/2/4, then re-tokenized) is NOT reproduced; the port keeps the raw netname and parses it directly via GiveItems.Apply — equivalent for the common (give-string) case, but spawnflag-prefixed (max/min/minus) target_items semantics and the GetResource-driven resource serialization are not honored. ; The loot-toucher delete branch (ITEM_IS_LOOT actor -> EXACTTRIGGER_TOUCH + de

[logic/audio] mapobject-target.speaker.ambient_triggered: ACTIVATOR (BIT3) still plays to ALL players, not just the triggering client — SpeakerUseActivator gates on IsRealClient (a bot/world activator produces nothing, matching QC's early-out) and emits on the stacking CH_TRIGGER channel, but there is no MSG_ONE / per-client sound facade, so every client hears it (documented TODO at TargetSpeaker.cs:211). ; The *-prefixed per-player voice-message sample now RESOLVES (ResolveNoise routes a *-name through Sounds.PlayerSoundSample + the wired ModelSoundRe

[logic] teleporters.core.telefrag: The !g_race && !g_cts && !(round_handler_IsActive() && !round_handler_IsRoundStarted()) suppression is STILL a STUB: TeleportRoundGateSuppressed() always returns false (Teleporters.cs:485), so telefrags still fire in Race/CTS and during the round countdown. The gate is structurally faithful and the seam exists, but the cross-file gametype/round-state accessor is not yet wired.

[logic] teleporters.trigger.touch_and_targets: RemoveGrapplingHooks(player) on teleport is still not called (teleport.qc:59-61) — a hooked player keeps the grappling hook through a teleporter. ; No EXACTTRIGGER_TOUCH brush-face precision test (box overlap only).

[logic] teleporters.spawn.entities: target_teleporter's spawn-time disambiguation (target_teleporter_checktarget: dest / self-target / normal teleporter) is still simplified to 'teleport activator to target on use'; the self-target (.enemy = this) case is not reproduced. ; teleport_findtarget bot-waypoint spawning (waypoint_spawnforteleporter) is omitted (bot navigation). ; noise precache (FOREACH_WORD precache_sound) is a no-op; harmless headless.

[presentation] portals.weapon.projectile: OnTouch re-probes the impact face with a worldonly traceline (ProbeImpact) to recover Q3 surface flags + the true plane normal, so the slick/clip + noimpact branches are reachable; but on a miss (no nearby solid) it falls back to the negated-velocity normal rather than the real wall normal. ; porto_v_angle held-angle capture for the non-secondary combined shot is dormant in stock play (g_balance_porto_secondary defaults to 1, so that branch is never reached); the hold state machine is modeled bu

[logic/values/timing/presentation/audio] portals.entity.lifecycle: Porto portals are realised as a seamless two-way warpzone, so there is no portal health (g_balance_portal_health=200) — Base portals can be shot down (Portal_Damage). ; No 15s portal lifetime (g_balance_portal_lifetime) / fade-out (SUB_SetFade time,0.5) / recharge-to-200+reset-fade on each use; port warpzone portals persist for the whole match (death/reset teardown is wired, but no time-based expiry). ; No portal model (MDL_PORTAL) / skins (0/1/2) or EF_RED / EF_BLUE|EF_STARDUST / EF_ADDITIVE gl

[logic/values/audio] portals.teleport.transform_and_amazing: ANNCE_ACHIEVEMENT_AMAZING on a portal telefrag within 1s of portal creation (portals.qc:193-194) is still never triggered — no teleport_time/tdeath_hit tracking on the warpzone path, and the warpzone crossing runs no tdeath at all. ; Independent-player gating (IS_INDEPENDENT_PLAYER: you can't walk through someone else's portal, portals.qc:307-314 / 486-487) is still absent. ; TELEPORT_FLAGS_PORTAL (FORCE_TDEATH) — the bitmask EXISTS (Teleporters.cs FlagsPortal) and TeleportPlayer honors it, but

[timing] portals.weapon.cleanup: Re-shooting after a COMPLETED pair does not tear down the prior pair: PlacePortoPortal keys _pendingPorto by (owner,portalId) and a fresh shot spawns new warpzones without disconnecting the previous fully-placed pair (Base Portal_SetInPortal disconnects + Portal_Remove the old). A player can still hold more than one live pair if they re-fire after one completes. ; No 15s time-based expiry (g_balance_portal_lifetime) on the warpzone path — a living player's portals persist until death/reset (see

[logic/values/timing] portals.weapon.hook_and_predict: Base Portal_Think predicts a player/hook crossing the portal plane THIS frame (Portal_WillHitPlane, PHYS_GRAVITY-aware tracebox over velocity*2*frametime) and teleports pre-emptively so fast movers don't tunnel; the warpzone realisation relies on a fixed trigger overlap, with no predict-ahead. (Warpzone.Teleport now has a same-frame finishtime guard and a plane-side test, but still no velocity*2*frametime predict-ahead.) ; A player's active grappling hook is still not teleported through the port

[logic/values] mapobject-triggers.multi.trigger_multiple: MultiTouch is MISSING the pressedkeys check (CS(toucher).pressedkeys & this.pressedkeys, Base multi.qc:135-137) — Entity.PressedKeys now EXISTS (written by the dodging mutator) but MultiTouch (Triggers.cs:156-158) never reads it AND the trigger's own this.pressedkeys parse-field is unported, so the gate is skipped and a key-gated trigger fires without the player holding the keys (the only remaining MultiTouch logic gap; the comment at the gate site documents the omission) ; CTS client-specific .

[presentation] mapobject-triggers.push.trigger_push: no animdecide_setaction(ANIMACTION_JUMP) jump pose on launch (jumppads.qc:402) — needs the cross-file anim seam ; no jumppadcount / jumppadsused multi-pad bookkeeping (jumppads.qc:379-405) — affects multi-jumppad kill-credit + the jump anim gate ; q3compat gravity correction (grav /= 750/800) and the q3 target_push 1.5s debounce not applied

[logic/values/liveness] mapobject-triggers.swamp.trigger_swamp: slowdown is STILL DEAD: SwampThink stamps it.SwampSlug + the 0.5 swamp_slowdown, but NO code in PlayerPhysics/MovementParameters reads SwampSlug.SwampSlowdown as maxspd_mod (Base player.qc:50: maxspd_mod *= (swampslug.active==ACTIVE_ACTIVE) ? swampslug.swamp_slowdown : 1). RE-CONFIRMED 2026-06-25: grep of src finds writers in Triggers.cs:696/720/744 + the field def (EntityMapObjectStateExtra.cs:86) only — no reader. Players are damaged but move at full speed.

[logic] mapobject-triggers.magicear.trigger_magicear: TUBA melody match branch (MAGICEAR_TUBA / W_Tuba_HasPlayed) out of scope — a TUBA ear returns msgin unchanged ; strdecolorize is a no-op (chat color codes minimal) so MAGICEAR_NODECOLORIZE is largely inert

[values/timing/presentation] mapobject-triggers.music.target_music: fade curve approximated: the client uses a two-AudioStreamPlayer A/B crossfade rather than QC's per-source state easing by frametime/fade_time (in) and frametime/fade_rate (out)

[presentation/liveness] mapobject-triggers.viewloc.trigger_viewlocation: no client 2.5D side-scroller camera consumer: the server stamps player.ViewLoc each frame but the view is never locked to the start->end line, so the camera region has no visible effect (unchanged this wave)

[audio] monster-golem.audio.voice_cues: Full data-driven .sounds-file parse + skin-keyed Monster_Sounds_Update is still hardcoded per-descriptor (SoundCues set + SoundCueCount switch mirror golem.dpm_0.sounds by hand) rather than parsed from the model's .sounds at load; behaviorally faithful for golem but the general GlobalSound/Monster_Sounds_Update plumbing is a named no-op. ; QC spamsound(SND_BODYIMPACT1) pain placeholder (when a monster has no pain sample) not played — n/a for golem (golem has pain samples).

[audio] mutator-nades.boom.dispatch: The colored explosion particle (Send_Effect_Except with per-type EFFECT + player-color min/max via findbetterlocation) is omitted (render-only). ; Base plays SND_Null on CH_SHOTS_SINGLE + SND_ROCKET_IMPACT on CH_SHOTS; the port plays only weapons/rocket_impact.wav.

[audio] mutator-nades.boom.napalm: g_nades_napalm_ball_damageforcescale (4) is not applied: napalm is modeled as the Burning status effect with no knockback, so the fireball/fountain damage-force push (QC proj.damageforcescale) is missing. ; Water-contents velocity damping (pointcontents CONTENT_WATER -> velocity*0.5 / z=200) and the round-not-started fountain/ball delete guard are omitted; EFFECT_FIREBALL_LASER trail + EF_FLAME ball effect omitted (render).

[audio] mutator-nades.boom.entrap_veil_darkness: Entrap MonsterMove run/walk slow is omitted (no MonsterMove hook chain in the port). ; Veil tint of enemies (render colormod) omitted; only the teammate alpha-hide is ported. Veil monster-alpha lapse omitted. ; Darkness blind overlay (HUD_DarkBlinking full-screen fill) + SND_BLIND are CSQC-only and missing; only the NadeDarknessTime deadline is set server-side.

[audio] mutator-nades.bonus.economy: GameRules_scoring_is_vip is treated as false: the VIP-kill medium bonus + flagcarrier time-score multiplier (g_nades_bonus_score_time_flagcarrier=2, scaled by KH key count) are not applied — the port always uses bonus_score_time. ; The bonus notification + SND(NADE_BONUS) on banking a nade are not emitted (presentation/audio).

[audio] mutator-nades.presentation.client: No nade projectile model/trail (nade_customize / nade_spawn CSQCProjectile + Nade_TrailEffect), no orb 3D model (orb_draw), no orb 2D full-screen color flash (orb_draw2d / hud_colorflash_alpha), no darkness blind overlay (HUD_DarkBlinking) + SND_BLIND, no spawn-loc EF_STARDUST render. ; No DrawAmmoNades (cl_nades.qc:86): the ammo-panel bonus-nade count + progress bar + (rainbow) type icon is not drawn. ; The held-nade charge ring (CrosshairPanel.NadeTimer) is wired + networked AND now functional

[audio] waypoints.itemstime.respawn_countdown: On the first countdown tick spawns WP_Weapon (weapon pickups) / WP_Item (else) attached to the item, UpdateBuildFinished drives the build-progress bar to the scheduled respawn, and every tick Pings the waypoint (radar ring) + plays ITEMRESPAWNCOUNTDOWN. SpectatorOnly items get SPRITERULE_SPECTATOR (the sv_itemstime 1/2 gate). ; audio: partial — QC soundto(MSG_ONE) plays ITEMRESPAWNCOUNTDOWN per visible client; the port's Common layer has no per-client soundto seam, so it emits a single positiona

[audio] nexball.weapon.ballstealer: WEP_NEXBALL / BallStealer weapon does not exist; there is no way to SHOOT or pass the carried ball. ; No primary launch, no secondary tackle projectile (W_Nexball_Touch ball-steal), no homing safe-pass (W_Nexball_Think). ; No weapon-arena handler, so Nexball is not enforced as a weapon-arena mode (carriers keep their normal weapons).
[audio] onslaught.cp.icon_think_regen: Damaged-FX sparks + ONS_SPARK1/2 ambient sounds not emitted (presentation/audio FX, not the steady regen logic).

[audio] onslaught.captureshield: No physical capture-shield entity around shielded generators/control points: an enemy player walks into a shielded node with no push-back, no GENERATOR/CONTROLPOINT_SHIELDED center notification on contact, and no spinning additive shield model. (The damage-ignore IS enforced via node.Shielded, and the weapon-damage blocked-by-shield SOUND hint IS played in IconDamage/GeneratorDamage; only the touch-push shield ENTITY + its center-print are missing.)

[audio] onslaught.spawn.teleport_choose: No control-point-to-control-point teleport (use key / click-radar), no respawn-at-chosen-CP, no spawn_at_controlpoints / spawn_at_generator placement near owned nodes. Players spawn only via the generic team spawn system. The ons_spawn client command + clickradar HUD are absent. (spawn_choose defaults ON in Base.) ONS_TELEPORT/ONS_TELEPORT_ANTISPAM notifications registered as data but never emitted.

[audio] onslaught.presentation.csqc: No generator/control-point/icon world models on the client; no progressive damage-model swaps; no icon bob/spin/punch-on-hit animation; no generator damage `frame`; no radar link lines; no objective death-cam (ENT_ONSCAMERA/WantEventchase/ons_camSetup) on round loss; no generator explosion sequence (rays/shockwave/gibs/electricity); no particle effects (EFFECT_SPARKS/CAP/RAGE/ROCKET_EXPLODE) or waypoint-sprite build/health bars.

[logic] physics-movetypes.push.pushmove: Base special-cases MOVETYPE_PHYSICS riders (push.qc:124-129): they just translate + relink and skip the PushEntity/blocked logic. The port has no such branch — a MOVETYPE_PHYSICS rider goes through the normal PushEntity sweep + stuck-check. Low impact (the port rarely uses MOVETYPE_PHYSICS riders). ; Base gathers riders via findradius over the whole live entity list (FOREACH_ENTITY_RADIUS); the port iterates Entities() with a swept-box prefilter — functionally equivalent result set, but a flat s

[logic/values/timing/presentation] physics-movetypes.matchticrate.interp: STILL OPEN: No Movetype_Physics_MatchTicrate equivalent and no tic_* snapshot fields on Entity (grep: zero hits in src/ for MatchTicrate/tic_origin/tic_velocity). Entities advance only on the fixed server tick, with no between-tick interpolation in this layer. In Base this smooths CSQC gibs/casings/projectiles/trains at render rate; whether visible in the port depends on its separate render-side interpolation (PredictionBuffer/ClientNet — out of this unit's scope).

[logic] physics-player.water.swim: Viewloc (1D-rail) water sub-cases not ported: physics.qc:269-270 sets wishvel.z = -160 when this.viewloc, and physics.qc:408-416 runs a SEPARATE accel path (plain addspeed accel, no PM_Accelerate QW pass) when in a viewloc. The port always runs the non-viewloc path. Inert on stock Xonotic maps (no 1D viewloc rails), so logic:partial not missing. ; Liveness of the swim path on a live water map not behaviorally confirmed this audit (depends on CheckWater correctly setting WaterLevel from map liqui

[audio] sv-client-lifecycle.frame.idle_kick: Idle players are never kicked or demoted to spectator; the idle countdown bangs (SND TALK2 at countdown_time), CENTER_DISCONNECT_IDLING / CENTER_MOVETOSPEC_IDLING centerprints, and INFO_QUIT_KICK_IDLING / INFO_MOVETOSPEC_IDLING notifications never appear. ; The alivetime parm_idlesince afk-gate (pause alivetime accrual after 30s idle) is not modeled, so xonstat skill-per-second would be skewed by afk time.

[audio] sv-client-lifecycle.frame.prethink_misc: UpdateChatBubble (the chatbubble.spr / minigame_busy bubble over a typing or minigame-busy player, ChatBubbleThink + ShowChatBubble hook) is not modeled — no visible 'is typing' bubble. ; The PlayerPreThink +use edge dispatch (PlayerUseKey) is not a single ported function: individual +use consumers (buttons/vehicles/ctf) read the button directly, but the usekeypressed edge tracker + PlayerUseKey routing (and its spectate/minigame branches) are scattered/absent. ; SetZoomState (server-side weapon
[audio] sv-commands-votes.vote.call: ANNCE_VOTE_CALL announce + the networked Nagger vote HUD are not emitted by this core (broadcast text only).

[audio] sv-commands-votes.vote.resolve: ANNCE_VOTE_ACCEPT / ANNCE_VOTE_FAIL announcer cues are not played (presentation/audio handled in host, not invoked here). ; VoteSpam drops the QC 'didn't have to vote' wording variant (always prints 'didn't vote'); cosmetic broadcast-text divergence.

[audio] sv-commands-votes.timeout.state_machine: Presentation/audio carried as chat-broadcast lines, not the QC MSG_CENTER per-client overwriting center-print nor the real ANNCE_PREPARE sound cue: the Godot-free core has no networked center-print/announcer channel here. The BEGINNING countdown is surfaced in full but the ENDING countdown is throttled to only the final-stretch lines (<= sv_timeout_resumetime+1) to avoid flooding chat. The state/timing is faithful; only the delivery medium differs.

[audio] sv-world-rules.checkrules.cascade: WinningCondition_RanOutOfSpawns (team spawn-exhaustion draw/win, g_spawn_useallspawns) is still not ported (confirmed absent, GameWorld.cs:2884 comment). ; leadlimit / leadlimit_and_fraglimit WIN semantics are still not evaluated in the world layer (the win comes from the gametype MatchEnded latch + tie report). The leadlimit math exists in GameScores.CountFragsRemaining but only feeds the frags-remaining announcer, not the end-of-match decision. ; The CheckRules_World mutator hook (M_ARGV statu

[audio] sv-world-rules.checkrules.overtime: STAT(OVERTIMES) is not networked to clients (the HUD OVERTIME banner has no server feed) — deferred per T42. ; race/qualifying race_StartCompleting branch deliberately omitted (those modes latch their own winner).

[audio] tka.ball.respawn: BallEntity.RandomMapLocation uses the QC SelectSpawnPoint FALLBACK path (a random spawnpoint), not the engine MoveToRandomMapLocation world-bounds sampler (rejecting solid/lava/sky). Faithful to the fallback QC itself drops to, but the primary random-world-point sampler is approximated. ; Respawn sound (SND_KA_RESPAWN, ATTEN_NONE) IS played in RespawnBall, but the EFFECT_KA_BALL_RESPAWN particles at the old+new origin remain deferred (the WP_KaBall waypoint IS now produced — see tka.waypoints.tr

[audio] turret-framework.projectile.generic: The generic TurretSpawn.Projectile helper itself leaves NetName empty (Generic trail); the proper proj_type trail only appears because every turret CALLER overwrites NetName after the spawn — a turret that forgot to stamp it would render trailless.

[audio] turret-framework.client.presentation: No tur_head sub-entity: the steady-state head MODEL rotation (turret_draw tur_head.angles += avelocity*dt) is never rendered, and the per-shot head-frame anim rides the turret body's own Entity.Frame instead of a separate head bone. ; Framework-wide low-HP feedback only partial: just EWheelTurret emits the <127 te_spark; the all-turret turret_draw sparks and the <85 SMOKE_LARGE / <32 SMOKE_SMALL smoke tiers are unported. ; No turret_draw2d radar layer: no waypoint sprite and no over-turret healt

[audio] turret-hk.weapon.player: The hidden player weapon WEP_HK (HunterKillerAttack) — the player branch of wr_think (weapon_prepareattack / turret_initparams / W_SetupShot_Dir / weapon_thinkf WFRAME_FIRE1) that lets a given-the-weapon player fire the same guided rocket — is not ported at all (no WEP_HK / HunterKillerAttack symbol exists in the port).

[audio] turret-machinegun.lifecycle.die_respawn: No client death FX: the non-NORESPAWN branch in Base turret_die does NO sound/effect (only the NORESPAWN branch plays SND_ROCKET_IMPACT + EFFECT_ROCKET_EXPLODE before delete — the port does port that NORESPAWN branch at Die:927-932); base/head gib toss lives in cl_turrets.qc (client). Port emits no client FX/gibs on the normal respawn path. Still open. ; Respawn restores state but does not re-stamp a head model / send a full CSQC update (TNSF_FULL_UPDATE) — no networking layer. Still open.

[audio] turret-plasma_dual.damage.gating_die_respawn: Respawn EF_NODRAW hide-frame split not modeled: Base hides (EF_NODRAW) at time+0.2 then turret_respawn at respawntime-0.2; port schedules Respawn directly at now+RespawnTime. NET respawn timing is identical (0.2 + (60-0.2) = 60 == now+60) — only the intermediate EF_NODRAW hide-frame (a presentation detail) is unmodeled. ; Death FX/sound (SND_ROCKET_IMPACT + EFFECT_ROCKET_EXPLODE) only fire in Base on TFL_DMG_DEATH_NORESPAWN; plasma_dual always respawns so this branch never applies (port has it c

(The Raptor's SEPARATE incoming-missile alarm at Raptor.cs:408 IS live.) ; Host-only feeder: UpdateVehicleHud reads LocalServerPlayer (host path). The cross-client VEHICLESTAT_* network

QC's per-tick

[audio] raptor.flight_controller: Engine fly sound (every 7.955812s) not played. ; No visible model pitch/roll/yaw pose beyond entity angles; player glue origin+'0 0 32' done but no cockpit/exterior model.

Raptor.Think:231 snapshots vehicle.OldVelocity BEFORE the tick's physics (matches Racer.Think:296), and Spawn:141 seeds PlayTime = Time to clear the impact debounce on respawn — so vehicles_impact computes

[values] warpzones.spawn.trigger: scale/modelscale and the warpzone_isboxy box-vs-exacttrigger distinction from WarpZoneLib_ExactTrigger_Init are not modeled; the EF_NODEPTHTEST render flag and WarpZone_Send CSQC serialisation are unported (no client entity). ; RE-AUDIT 2026-06-25: the port registers ONLY the spawnfunc names `trigger_warpzone` and `trigger_warpzone_position` (MapObjectsRegistry.cs:110-111). Base's CANONICAL position spawnfunc is `misc_warpzone_position` (server.qc:642); `trigger_warpzone_position` merely delegat

[presentation] warpzones.porto: Porto portals share the missing client portal render — they teleport correctly server-side but show as dark placeholders, same as map warpzones. ; The Amazing-on-portal-telefrag announcer (portals.qc:192-194, ANNCE_ACHIEVEMENT_AMAZING within 1s of portal creation) needs the tdeath path the warpzone crossing does not run — see the portals.teleport unresolved gap noted inline (Warpzone.cs:262). ; The +/-48 PortoMins/Maxs trigger box is a port choice; Base Porto portal bounds not re-verified.

[logic/values/timing/presentation] warpzones.client.render: No Godot SubViewport+Camera3D renders the linked OUT side onto the portal surface; HeroMaterials.BuildPortal (HeroMaterials.cs:168) is a static dark near-black StandardMaterial3D mirror. The warpzone reads as an opaque/wavy wall — you cannot see through it. PVS-fade and the r_water enable HACK are also absent.

[logic/values/timing/presentation] warpzones.client.fixview: No client view rotate when the camera straddles a zone, no near-clip push-out (geometry clips at the seam), no roll-kill on a roll-changing crossing, no exterior-model hide. Crossing a warpzone is visually jarring.

[logic/timing/presentation] warpzones.client.teleported_view: Server never sends a warpzone_teleported entity and never re-rotates v_angle (WarpZone_PlayerPhysics_FixVAngle); the client never rotates VF_CL_VIEWANGLES / CL_RotateMoves on a crossing. The view does not carry through the seam.

[presentation] warpzones.client.prediction: Render/view side of the crossing still missing: a predicted warp relocates the carrier in lockstep with authority (no rubber-band) and stamps .fixangle, but the seamless view-rotate/portal render (warpzones.client.render / .fixview / .teleported_view) is unported — the camera still snaps visually across the seam even though the origin no longer corrects.

[logic/values/timing/presentation] warpzones.camera: No func_camera spawnfunc; security-camera dpcamera surfaces render as a dark mirror placeholder (HeroMaterials) rather than a fixed camera view of the target position.

[logic/timing] warpzones.reconnect: Link()/InitMapZones() run once at boot and never re-derive. Maps with moving warpzones (spawnflag 1 -> WarpZone_Think) or runtime trigger_warpzone_reconnect/target_warpzone_reconnect will have stale transforms.

[logic/timing] warpzones.observer_warp: Observers / SOLID_NOT clients are warped through zones each frame by WarpZone_StartFrame (WarpZone_Find + WarpZone_Teleport(-1,0), no targets) and by the teleportable-simple-teleporter pass; unported, so spectators cannot pass through warpzones. WarpZone_StoreProjectileData per-frame snapshot also absent.

[logic/presentation] warpzones.combat.traceextras: Grenade/toss trajectory prediction (WarpZone_TraceToss) and projectile particle trails (WarpZone_TrailParticles[_WithMultiplier]) do not bend through portals — bot grenade aim and trail visuals are wrong across a warpzone. Minor.

[audio] rifle.audio.ric: MINOR channel mismatch: Base plays SND_RIC_RANDOM on CH_SHOTS; the port's PlayRic->PlayOn uses the RIC sound's default channel hint (Weapon), not CH_SHOTS. Inaudible difference in practice (affects only same-channel sound cutoff), but not byte-faithful.

[audio] weapon-tuba.audio.note_sound: No pitch-step crossfade / sound7 speed shift: Base rounds note to the nearest multiple-of-step sample AND crossfades to its neighbour while pitch-shifting the remainder m via sound7; the port now correctly snaps to the nearest recorded sample (PitchStepNote) so a real file always plays, but it plays that one sample flat — the residual pitch is not shifted. ; No sustained note: the port fires one static one-shot per blast tick (~0.05s) rather than a held loop that fades on release (cl_tuba_fadeti
